semanticCore: Subduing, striking, subduing-clubs, maces, iron collars, or funnel shape

keyDerivations.meaning: Iron club, hooked rod, mace, or subduing instrument used for punishment

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَلَهُمْ مَقَامِعُ مِنْ حَدِيدٍ (22:21): And for them are hooked maces/clubs of iron (for physical punishment in Hell).
summaryEn: The root represents subduing by force or striking (Qam'), describing the iron maces designed to strike and subdue inhabitants of Hell whenever they try to escape.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الضرب بمقامع الحديد لردع المعتدي وقهر الخصم وإذلاله (قمع)، واصفاً أدوات العذاب ومطارق الحديد المعدة لضرب مستحقي النار.
rawText: قَمَعَهُ 1 : see قَمَأَهُ. قَمِعُ البُسْرِ What sticks to the date, around its stalk: (Mgh:) the base of the date. (Mgh, art. ذنب.) See ثُفْرُوقٌ. === See also a use of the pl. أَقْمَاع, voce دَرْدَار. === قِمَعُ الأُذُونِ The meatus of the ear: see جُلْجُلاَنٌ.
